summary: The conjecture that the bound $h(n, k, W_{2r+1}) \leq c_r n^2/k^{2-1/(r+1)}$ is tight up to a constant factor appears to remain open as of May 2026. A survey of all 7 papers citing arXiv:2102.10220 via the Semantic Scholar API reveals no follow-up that establishes a matching lower bound for odd wheels; the citing literature focuses instead on MaxCut and odd-cycle-free graph structure. The sole internal reference (arXiv:2105.03956, Scott–Seymour–Spirkl 2022) is a clear mismatch: that paper proves a Fox conjecture about pure pairs in perfect graphs, which is unrelated to $h(n,k,W_{2r+1})$.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch of https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.03956 confirms this paper is about pure pairs in perfect graphs (proves a Fox conjecture on pure pairs with |A|,|B| >= n^{1-c}). It does NOT address h(n,k,W_{2r+1}) or the sharpness of Theorem 1.5. The attributed contribution text was a false positive from the extraction pipeline.

search_queries: h(n,k,W_{2r+1}) odd wheel H-free k-colorable Fox Himwich Mani 2021 tight bound conjecture
Fox Himwich Mani "H-free graph k-colorable" 2102.10220 odd wheel cite 2022 2023 2024
Semantic Scholar API citations for arXiv:2102.10220
notes: No follow-up proving or disproving the odd-wheel tightness conjecture was found among the 7 known citing papers (retrieved via Semantic Scholar API). The conjecture is open with high confidence: the paper is ~5 years old, has a modest citation count, and the closest citing work addresses MaxCut rather than the $h(n,k,H)$ function for wheels.
